task: T7 - Implement outbound forensic logging with trace linkage
summary: All 4 outbound TCP write points in server.py are instrumented with forensic logging. Each outbound frame is captured with full schema-compliant fields before wire transmission.

instrumented_write_points.context: END frame after per-device ACK_SETTING (state machine path)
END frame after legacy ACK_SETTING (single-slot fallback)
Normal ACK/response from _generate_ack (covers: ack_getactual, ack_bare, end_time, end_bare, setting_delivery)

fields.request_id: Auto-generated as conn_{conn_id}_{seq} if not supplied
fields.conn_id: Integer connection identifier from server connection counter
fields.raw_xml: Complete unmodified XML payload including CRC tag and CRLF
fields.crc: CRC32 hex of raw_xml (8-char lowercase hex)
fields.state_before: Populated for setting-related frames with state machine context

non_blocking_design.hot_path_impact: Zero synchronous disk I/O on write path; buffer flushes asynchronously or on connection close

trace_linkage.inbound_to_outbound: conn_id + request_id correlate inbound trigger to outbound response
trace_linkage.trigger_context: trigger.table and trigger.result identify which inbound frame caused the outbound response

output_file: DATA_DIR/forensic_outbound.log (JSONL format, one record per line)
